返回本批次

在巴哈马鲨鱼体内发现咖啡因、可卡因和止痛药

Caffeine, cocaine, and painkillers detected in sharks from The Bahamas

www.sciencedirect.com 作者 LostMyLogin 分数 75 评论 53 2026/04/13 16:13
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 简介药品和个人护理产品 (PPCP)，包括非甾体抗炎药 (NSAID)、镇痛药和精神活性物质，越来越多地被认为是水生环境中的新兴污染物 (CEC)，主要通过废水排放、农业径流和城市排放进入海洋生态系统（Nishmitha 等，2025）。其中许多化合物具有持久性、生物活性，具有影响非目标生物的潜力，并且经常在世界各地的沿海水域中检测到（Świacka 等人，2021），引起人们对其生态后果的担忧，特别是在生物多样性和脆弱的海洋系统中。尽管地表水、沉积物和生物群中 CEC 的记录不断增加，但研究仍然不成比例地集中于淡水生态系统和模式物种，例如无脊椎动物和硬骨鱼（Koumaki 等人）等人，2017；Rastogi 等人，2021；Huynh 等人，2023）。相比之下，软骨鱼类（鲨鱼、鳐鱼和鳐鱼）的生态毒理学 CEC 研究在很大程度上被忽视（Mezzelani 和 Regoli，2022；Świacka 等，2022；de Farias Araujo 等，2024；Hauser-Davis 等，2024；Punginelli 等，2024）。考虑到软骨鱼作为中型到顶级捕食者的生态作用，以及它们典型的缓慢生活史和越来越多地接触人类改变的沿海栖息地，这种分类差距尤其令人担忧。这些特征，再加上渔业是对其保护的主要威胁（Dulvy 等人，2021），使它们特别容易受到额外压力的影响。关于 CEC 如何与软骨鱼独特的生理和解毒系统相互作用的信息非常有限，这是评估生态风险和种群影响的主要障碍，阻碍了为这一高危群体制定有效的保护策略。这种知识差距在经历快速沿海发展和人为压力增加的地区尤其重要，例如巴哈马群岛，旅游业是该地区的主要经济驱动力并持续扩张（Rolle 等人，2020）。游客的不断涌入，加上度假屋和出租物业数量的增加，显着增加了当地废水的体积和化学复杂性（Lichtmannegger 等，2024；Rodríguez-Alcántara 等，2024；Wosnick 等，2024）。这些压力可能导致污染物（包括 CEC）持续进入近岸海洋环境。许多鲨鱼物种栖息在这些浅海沿海地区，可能会增加它们接触这些化合物的机会。在巴哈马，这些动物的存在还具有重大的文化和经济重要性，特别是通过旅游业（Haas 等，2017）。因此，了解这些污染物如何影响鲨鱼生理和长期种群健康变得至关重要，这不仅是为了保护沿海生态系统的关键生态组成部分，也是为了保护它们提供的社会和经济效益。然而，迄今为止，有关这些物种 CEC 的生态毒理学研究实际上仍然缺乏，这代表了该地区保护和环境管理工作的一个关键盲点。在这种情况下，这项研究提供了对 CEC 的首次评估，涵盖广泛的药理和化学类别，包括镇痛药、非甾体抗炎药 (NSAID)、抗生素、抗惊厥药、抗抑郁药、杀菌剂和精神活性物质，特别是对乙酰氨基酚、苯甲酰爱康宁、咖啡因、卡马西平、环丙沙星、西酞普兰、克林霉素、可卡因、双氯芬酸、氟虫腈、氟西汀、尼美舒利、吡罗昔康、舍曲林、磺胺甲恶唑、磺胺吡啶、三氯生、甲氧苄氨嘧啶和曲马多，存在于伊柳塞拉岛南部和中部捕获的自由放养鲨鱼的血清中，巴哈马。目的是在一个通常被视为原始且受人为污染影响最小的地区确定这些化合物的基线浓度。除了筛选药物和兴奋剂之外，还分析了一套常用于评估脊椎动物健康和生理应激的生化标记物。这些包括甘油三酯、总胆固醇、磷、乳酸和尿素，它们共同提供了对代谢、渗透、肾和肝功能的深入了解（Gallagher 等，2017；Morón-Elorza 等，2022；Starostinetsky-Malonek 等，2023）。本研究中筛选的许多化合物已知会影响哺乳动物和其他动物的这些生理途径。脊椎动物。例如，一些非甾体抗炎药（如双氯芬酸）与尿素和磷水平改变所反映的肾损伤有关（Abiola等人，2019年；Drożdżal等人，2021年），而某些兴奋剂（如可卡因和咖啡因）与高血糖、乳酸积累和脂质代谢破坏有关（Han等人，1996年；Drożdżal等人，2021年）。吴等人，2025）；对乙酰氨基酚具有肝毒性，通常会影响血脂调节（Mazaleuskaya 等，2015；Ramachandran 和 Jaeschke，2019；Mizogami 和 Tsuchiya，2022）。虽然没有详尽列出所有潜在的药物效应，但该标记套件提供了一个综合框架，能够检测可能接触 CEC 的鲨鱼的生理变化。通过您的组织访问通过您的组织登录检查对全文的访问。通过您的组织访问部分片段鲨鱼捕获来自五个物种的成年鲨鱼，即柠檬鲨 (Negaprion brevirostris, n = 33)、大西洋护士鲨 (Ginglymostoma cirratum, n = 8）、黑鳍鲨（Carcharhinuslimbatus，n = 8）、加勒比礁鲨（Carcharhinus perezi，n = 23）和虎鲨（Galeocerdo cuvier，n = 2）是在伊柳塞拉岛沿海地区使用延绳钓捕获的。使用围网捕获幼年柠檬鲨 (n = 9) 和大西洋护士鲨 (n = 2)。对于围网捕获的幼年鲨鱼，结果在 85 条分析的鲨鱼中，有 28 条表现出了四种 CEC 的可检测水平：对乙酰氨基酚、双氯芬酸、可卡因和咖啡因（表 1）。所有其他化合物均低于分析样品中的 LOD。检测仅限于三个物种：真鲨、Ginglymostoma cirratum 和短吻鲨。在真鲨中，7 只个体中检测到双氯芬酸 (1.47–14.85 ng mL−1)，2 只个体中检测到对乙酰氨基酚（4.29 和 11.63 ng mL−1），1 只个体中检测到可卡因（2.12 ng mL−1）。咖啡因讨论 据我们所知，这是在全球任何鲨鱼物种中检测到咖啡因和对乙酰氨基酚的第一份报告，也是在巴哈马群岛（通常被称为原始环境）的鲨鱼中检测到双氯芬酸和可卡因的第一份报告。为了将这些发现结合起来，表 3 总结了之前发表的报告软骨鱼中存在 CEC 的研究。检测到的 CEC 代表了一组不同的生物活性化合物，有可能干扰 CRediT 作者贡献声明Natascha Wosnick：写作 - 审稿和编辑、写作 - 初稿、可视化、验证、监督、项目管理、方法、调查、资金获取、形式分析、数据管理、概念化。 Mónica Alejandra Herrera Agudelo：验证、方法论、形式分析、数据管理。 Carolina Turnes Pasini Deolindo：验证、方法论、形式分析、数据管理。罗德里戈·霍夫：方法论、形式分析、数据管理。 Luan Valdemiro Alves de AI 声明在准备本手稿期间，作者使用 ChatGPT (OpenAI) 来增强清晰度、语法和整体可读性。所有内容随后均经过审查、必要时修订，并由作者批准，作者对工作的完整性和准确性承担全部责任。竞争利益声明作者声明，他们不存在可能影响本文报告工作的已知竞争经济利益或个人关系。致谢NW 感谢岛屿学校和伊柳塞拉角研究所的后勤支持，以及协助现场活动的所有工作人员。 NW 还感谢环境规划和保护部 (DEPP) 和海洋资源部 (DMR) 授予研究许可。特别感谢理学学士 Khadejah Thompson 女士。 Candice Webb-Russell 和 Suzette I. Hepburn-Lyn 博士在审查© 2026 Elsevier Ltd 时提供的宝贵支持。保留所有权利，包括文本和数据挖掘、人工智能培训和类似技术的权利。

Original Summary

  1. IntroductionPharmaceuticals and personal care products (PPCPs), including non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), analgesics, and psychoactive substances, are increasingly recognized as contaminants of emerging concern (CECs) in aquatic environments, entering marine ecosystems primarily through wastewater effluents, agricultural runoff, and urban discharges (Nishmitha et al., 2025). Many of these compounds are persistent, bioactive, display the potential to affect non-target organisms and have been frequently detected in coastal waters worldwide (Świacka et al., 2021), raising concerns about their ecological consequences, particularly in biodiverse and vulnerable marine systems.Despite growing documentation of CECs in surface waters, sediment and biota, research remains disproportionately focused on freshwater ecosystems and model species, such as invertebrates and teleost fish (Koumaki et al., 2017; Rastogi et al., 2021; Huynh et al., 2023). In contrast, ecotoxicological CEC studies are largely neglected in elasmobranchs (sharks, rays, and skates) (Mezzelani and Regoli, 2022; Świacka et al., 2022; de Farias Araujo et al., 2024; Hauser-Davis et al., 2024; Punginelli et al., 2024). This taxonomic gap is particularly concerning given the ecological roles of elasmobranchs as meso-to apex predators, as well as their typically slow life histories and increasing exposure to human-altered coastal habitats. These characteristics, coupled with fisheries as the main threat to their conservation (Dulvy et al., 2021), make them particularly vulnerable to additional stressors. The very limited information on how CECs interact with the unique physiology and detoxification systems of elasmobranchs represents a major barrier to assessing ecological risks and population-level impacts, hindering the development of effective conservation strategies for this already high-at-risk group.This knowledge gap is particularly relevant in regions experiencing rapid coastal development and increased anthropogenic pressure, such as The Bahamas, where tourism is a major economic driver and continues to expand (Rolle et al., 2020). A growing influx of tourists, coupled with an increase in the number of vacation homes and rental properties, significantly heightens the volume and chemical complexity of local wastewater (Lichtmannegger et al., 2024; Rodríguez-Alcántara et al., 2024; Wosnick et al., 2024). These pressures can lead to the continuous input of pollutants, including CECs, into nearshore marine environments. Many shark species inhabit these same shallow, coastal zones, potentially increasing their exposure to these compounds. In The Bahamas, the presence of these animals also carries significant cultural and economic importance, particularly through the tourism sector (Haas et al., 2017). Thus, understanding how these contaminants affect shark physiology and long-term population health becomes paramount, not only to safeguard a key ecological component of coastal ecosystems, but also to preserve the social and economic benefits they provide. To date, however, ecotoxicological research concerning CECs in these species remains virtually absent, representing a critical blind spot in conservation and environmental management efforts in the region.In this context, this study provides the first assessment on CECs, encompassing a broad spectrum of pharmacological and chemical classes, including analgesics, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), antibiotics, anticonvulsants, antidepressants, biocides and psychoactive substances, specifically acetaminophen, benzoylecgonine, caffeine, carbamazepine, ciprofloxacin, citalopram, clindamycin, cocaine, diclofenac, fipronil, fluoxetine, nimesulide, piroxicam, sertraline, sulfamethoxazole, sulfapyridine, triclosan, trimethoprim, and tramadol, in the blood serum of free-ranging sharks captured in south and central Eleuthera Island, The Bahamas. The aim was to establish baseline concentrations of these compounds in a region often regarded as pristine and minimally affected by anthropogenic pollution. In addition to screening for pharmaceuticals and stimulants, a suite of biochemical markers commonly used to assess vertebrate health and physiological stress was also analyzed. These included triglycerides, total cholesterol, phosphorus, lactate, and urea, which together provide insights into metabolic, osmotic, renal, and hepatic function (Gallagher et al., 2017; Morón-Elorza et al., 2022; Starostinetsky-Malonek et al., 2023).Many of the compounds screened in this study are known to influence these physiological pathways in mammals and other vertebrates. For example, some NSAIDS, like diclofenac, are associated with renal impairment reflected in altered urea and phosphorus levels (Abiola et al., 2019; Drożdżal et al., 2021), while certain stimulants such as cocaine and caffeine are linked to hyperglycemia, lactate accumulation, and disruptions in lipid metabolism (Han et al., 1996; Wu et al., 2025); and acetaminophen is hepatotoxic, often impacting lipid regulation (Mazaleuskaya et al., 2015; Ramachandran and Jaeschke, 2019; Mizogami and Tsuchiya, 2022). Although not exhaustive of all potential drug effects, this marker suite provides an integrative framework able to detect physiological alterations in sharks potentially exposed to CECs.Access through your organizationCheck access to the full text by signing in through your organization.Access through your organizationSection snippetsShark capturesAdult sharks from five species, namely Lemon sharks (Negaprion brevirostris, n = 33), Atlantic Nurse sharks (Ginglymostoma cirratum, n = 8), Blacktip sharks (Carcharhinus limbatus, n = 8), Caribbean Reef sharks (Carcharhinus perezi, n = 23), and Tiger sharks (Galeocerdo cuvier, n = 2), were captured using longlines in coastal areas of Eleuthera Island. Juvenile Lemon sharks (n = 9) and Atlantic Nurse sharks (n = 2) were captured using seine nets. For seine net captures, juvenile sharks wereResultsOf the 85 analyzed sharks, 28 individuals exhibited detectable levels of four CECs: acetaminophen, diclofenac, cocaine, and caffeine (Table 1). All other compounds were below the LOD in the analyzed samples. Detections were restricted to three species: Carcharhinus perezi, Ginglymostoma cirratum, and Negaprion brevirostris.In C. perezi, diclofenac was detected in seven individuals (1.47–14.85 ng mL−1), acetaminophen in two (4.29 and 11.63 ng mL−1), and cocaine in one (2.12 ng mL−1). CaffeineDiscussionTo the best of our knowledge, this is the first report of caffeine and acetaminophen detected in any shark species worldwide, and the first report of diclofenac and cocaine in sharks from The Bahamas, an environment commonly described as pristine. To contextualize these findings, Table 3 summarizes previously published studies reporting the presence of CECs in elasmobranchs.The detected CECs represent a diverse group of biologically active compounds with the potential to interfere withCRediT authorship contribution statementNatascha Wosnick: Writing – review & editing, Writing – original draft, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Project administration, Methodology, Investigation, Funding acquisition, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Mónica Alejandra Herrera Agudelo: Validation, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation. Carolina Turnes Pasini Deolindo: Validation, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation. Rodrigo Hoff: Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation. Luan Valdemiro Alves de AI declarationDuring the preparation of this manuscript, the authors used ChatGPT (OpenAI) to enhance clarity, grammar, and overall readability. All content was subsequently reviewed, revised as necessary, and approved by the authors, who assume full responsibility for the integrity and accuracy of the work.Declaration of competing interestThe authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.AcknowledgmentsNW would like to thank The Island School and the Cape Eleuthera Institute for their logistical support, as well as all staff members who assisted with field activities. NW also extends gratitude to the Department of Environmental Planning and Protection (DEPP) and the Department of Marine Resources (DMR) for granting the research permits. A special thank you is due to Ms. Khadejah Thompson, B.Sc. Candice Webb-Russell, and Dr. Suzette I. Hepburn-Lyn for their invaluable support in reviewing the© 2026 Elsevier Ltd. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.

这将可卡因鲨鱼归类为纪录片 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cocaine_Shark
This classifies Cocaine Shark as a documentary https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cocaine_Shark

dbbk 2026/04/13 19:39
“这部电影讲述了各种变异生物，但正如片名所暗示的那样，这些生物实际上都不是吸食可卡因的鲨鱼。”他们只有一份工作
"The film features various mutated creatures, none of which are actually sharks on cocaine as the title suggests." they had one job

butlike 2026/04/13 21:24

One. Job.

api 2026/04/13 19:57
该系列的潜力巨大。可卡因驼鹿、可卡因蛇（或可卡因蛇，其中可能有这样的台词“我想要这些该死的蛇妈妈远离可卡因妈妈！”）、可卡因狮子、可卡因河马、可卡因鳄鱼……
So much potential in that franchise. Cocaine moose, cocaine snake (or Snakes on Cocaine, which could have the line “I want these mother f’ing snakes off mother f’ing cocaine!”), cocaine lion, cocaine hippo, cocaine alligator…

oniony 2026/04/13 20:25
甚至是可卡因熊： https://m.imdb.com/title/tt14209916
Or even Cocaine Bear: https://m.imdb.com/title/tt14209916

JohnHaugeland 2026/04/13 20:34
这就是你回复的人所指的
that's what the person you're replying to was referring to
在 HN 中查看
snapplebobapple 2026/04/13 21:19
嗯，我认为扩展到不同的动物是一个错误。他们本可以从喜剧演变为戏剧，而不是在续集中把熊放在冰毒上。跟随这只熊经历它牙齿脱落的螺旋式下降，最终跌入谷底，喝自己的尿液来保持高处。救赎方舟可能与第一部电影中被它咬伤的露营者建立了一种不太可能的友谊，这是由这只熊清醒过来并进行道歉之旅引发的。
Meh, i think branching out to different animals is a mistake. They could have evolved beyond comedy to drama by instead putting the bear on meth in the sequel. Follow the bear through its downward spiral losing its teeth and eventually hitting bottom drinking its own urine to stay high. The redemption ark could be an unlikely friendship formed with one of the campers it mauled in the first movie sparked by the bear sobering up and doing the apology tour.
在 HN 中查看
api 2026/04/13 21:53
这听起来更像是神奇蘑菇熊，其中熊意识到所有生命都是一个，伤害其他生物就是伤害自己。
That sounds more like magic mushrooms bear, in which the bear realizes all life is one and to maul other beings is to maul yourself.
在 HN 中查看
yruam001 2026/04/13 18:02
的确
indeed
在 HN 中查看
numpad0 2026/04/13 20:08
并不奇怪。许多这些物质得到 代谢的，或者只是用水稀释并过滤掉，而不像神奇地解构并变成所有CO2和H2O和N2。因此，人类消耗的许多古怪的化合物往往会在人类聚集和排放体液并最终到达海洋的地方被发现。这并不立即表明例如邪恶的公司正在倾倒有毒废物，永远的塑料碎片会渗出可怕的添加剂，等等。
Not surprising. A lot of these substances get metabolized, or just diluted by water and filtered out without being like, magically deconstructed and turned into all CO2 and H2O and N2. So a lot of wacky chemical compounds humans tend to consume tend to get detected wherever humans gather and discharge bodily fluids that eventually reach the ocean. This does not immediately indicate that e.g. evil corporations are dumping toxic wastes, forever plastics pieces are leaching out scary additives, etc.
在 HN 中查看
augusto-moura 2026/04/13 23:14
嗯，我不相信这是人类排泄物的污染。人类摄入的咖啡因和止痛药的量应该太少。此外，身体确实会分解咖啡因和止痛药，废物量应该没有意义

如果在海洋中稀释，我会说它是无法检测的。可卡因更难，因为它不被普遍消费，它是针对一群人的，但不足以统计

Hm, I'm not convinced this is contamination from human waste. The quantity of caffeine and painkillers a human consume should be too small. Also, the body does break caffeine and painkillers, the amount in waste shouldn't be meaningful

If diluted in the oceans I would say that it would be undectable. Cocaine is even harder, because it is not commonly consumed, it is for a group of people, but not enough for the statistics

在 HN 中查看
croemer 2026/04/13 19:14
血液中测得的浓度约为 1-20 ng/mL。人类服用这些药物时的 Cmax 大约高 100-1000 倍。

我不会过分重视那些检测到的人具有不同的尿素/乳酸等的发现。可能有一些潜在的东西可以解释药物和生理学，比如年龄。

长期接触如此低的水平仍然可能是不好的——而且鱼类的生理机能也不同。

The measured concentrations are on the order of 1-20 ng/mL in blood. Cmax in humans when taking those drugs are about 100-1000x higher.

I wouldn't put too much weight on the finding that those with detections had different urea/lactate etc. There might be something underlying explaining both drug and physiology, like age.

Could still be bad to have chronic exposure at such low levels - also fish physiology is different.

在 HN 中查看
laughing_man 2026/04/13 18:36
我想知道这在多大程度上只是因为我们的测试变得更加敏感。
I wonder how much of this is just that our tests are getting more sensitive.
在 HN 中查看
croemer 2026/04/13 18:50
确切地说，检测本身并没有任何意义。剂量有关系吗？如果没有，那就不是。而且剂量可能不相关。
Exactly, the detection itself doesn't mean anything. Is the dose relevant? If not, then not. And the dose likely isn't relevant.
在 HN 中查看
eggy 2026/04/13 21:16
同意。当你放大时，即使是正常的生活事物也会让你担忧。我向我的孩子们展示了哪些生物生活在他们和其他人的身体上。您的毛囊和皮脂腺中生活着数以百万计的微小蜘蛛，称为蠕形螨，尤其是在您的脸上。当我在网上视频和图片中向我的孩子们展示这一事实时，我的妻子用邪恶的眼神看着我。虽然这些是生命的共生/寄生关系，但你看得越仔细，你看到的就越多！
Agreed. When you zoom in, even the normal life stuff can give you concern. I showed my kids what creatures live on their and others' bodies. You have millions of microscopic arachnids called Demodex mites living in your hair follicles and sebaceous glands, particularly on your face. My wife gave me an evil look as I showed my children this fact in online vids and pics. Granted these are symbiotic/parasitic relationships of life, but still, the closer you look, the more you see!
在 HN 中查看
doubleg72 2026/04/14 11:35
不是数百万
Not millions

mistrial9 2026/04/13 22:52

stavros 2026/04/13 18:05
继前几天的“塑料手套”确凿证据之后，我想知道这是否是实验室污染影响结果的又一个例子。
After the "plastic glove" smoking gun the other day, I wonder if this is another instance of lab contamination making it into the results.
在 HN 中查看
donkey_brains 2026/04/13 18:52
与塑料手套不同，研究人员通常不会将这些物质带入实验室。
Unlike plastic gloves, researchers don’t typically bring these substances into the lab.
